channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude. — 67 replies — 82.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude.

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-06-15 22:26 UTC

OP

Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude. If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.

https://worldofclaudecraft.com/

Early replies (first 3)

62362742
oldschool runescape is free

62362747
looks like shit
>If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.
sure if you can scam some subhumans but you're competing with 1 billion jeet scammers

62362749
>>62362737
Buy
An
Ad
!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62364408
>>62364390
What's he get paid?

62364447
>>62362737
IMAGINE OSRS 2.0 holy

62364461
>>62362843
>>62362844
it's so over for snailcat retards

62364719
>>62362737
impressive but 7 fps isn't going to work.

62364883
>>62364719
>7FPS isn't going to work
I bet you dont have hardware acceleration on dumbie

62364978
>>62363623
the assets are free open source models and stuff so it looks like shit

The gameplay is a classic wow clone, it's shockingly similar

62365021
>>62364090
Thanks...these dumbfucks thinks it cool they reskinned something and made it gay

62365211
>>62362737
>oneshotted
100% guarantee it wasn't actually "oneshotted". Also Fable 5 got banned already, even if you could one shot, it's over now.

62365245
>>62362737
this is trash

62365483
>>62362737
Dune banned AI for a reason.

62365496
>>62365211
/loop until complete

62365514
https://github.com/levy-street/world-of-claudecraft/blob/main/src/game/interactions.ts

>if if else if if else if if else else if if if else if

it's claude code alright

62365881
>>62362737
>Runescape-quality
so it's utter unplayable dogshit?
wow AI is the future

62365955
>>62362737
>>62362753
>>62363118
Globo-Homo Of SlopCraft

62365991
>>62362737
Very interesting. Not the project though, any starter $10 RPG template is a more complete game with less bugs. It's a completely useless code base worth $0. It's only impressive to people who understand nothing about game development. But it's interesting that OP hired spam bots for promotion. Is he somehow making money off of it?

62366047
>>62362742
>>62363625
in the shitty art style
also runescape is ultrashit skinner box

62366586
>>62364357
FUCK YEAH UTANI NIGGER

62366746
>>62362737
If you rip an existing open source game yourself rather than ask Claude to do it, you get documentation to go along with it.

62366802
>>62362737
Claude made me a trading bot for LINK. I am now down 99%. Thanks, claude!

62366858
>>62366802

perfect, 2 mistakes in one go!
>/biz/ dream

62367257
>>62366746
Or you just ask claude to create documentation along with the code and you don't get in trouble with the original creators.

62367267
>>62362775
It's so funny to see all the people boasting 10000x productivity and yet nothing changed. Actually everything is worsening, again and again.

62368083
>>62362737
hard pass.

62368165
If you pay attention to this guys comment
>>62363356

Tested it. My machine is not too powerful,
and was able to make some few quests.

For me it really feels like this comment: >>62364978
>The gameplay is a classic wow clone, it's shockingly similar
I'm a bit impressed! If he did not have to do a lot by himself,
imagine how much you can achieve by what little effort!!!

>This was made in 2 days and 91% of the Max 20x plan,
>as the author stated on the Reddit thread,
>so roughly ~$200.
>Supposedly, existing free assets were used and weren't generated.

Found a youtube video, where the game runs smoothly!
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cNzI3Ll2lHA

62368174
>>62368083
I would argue because the strafe on Q and E, and you could always change it if you wanted.

62369541
>>62362775
Cope

62370167
>>62365514
how do you think assembly works anon?

62370371
>>62365514
that is common for video game code. because all of the logic is happening in a while loop, you will almost only see if else control flows, quite often nested, and a for loop every once in a while.

62372596
>>62366047
soulless zoomgroid detected

62372603
>>62362775
and yet millions of goycattle have been laid off and it will only ever get worse (for you) :DD

item.title: I talked to two people today about rent. — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I talked to two people today about rent.

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-06-15 23:14 UTC

OP

I talked to two people today about rent.

The first guy said he lives in Atlanta and pays about $2,300 a month in rent. I mentioned that works out to roughly $27,600 a year, which I personally think is a lot for rent. He also told me he was let go from a sales job at a small company about two weeks ago. Small company = low pay. When I commented on the rent being high, he responded by saying, “maybe it is a lot for you.”

I told him it is a terrible financial decision.
I also told him that I live in a major city and my monthly taxes alone are more than that.

Later, I spoke with another guy who pays around $2,000 a month for a luxury apartment. That’s about $24,000 a year. I didn’t ask about his income or job situation, but I shared that I thought the annual rent cost seemed very high.

I kept telling him his rent was a waste of money and he should buy a house. He told me he might not want to to live somewhere for so long. I asked him how he expected to build wealth. The conversation went back and forth with me saying it was a waste of “money down the drain.” He eventually admitted it was hard and bad use of money and insulted me with “life is hard when YOU don’t live in mommies basement.”
Wow.
He is literally throwing money down the drain and I trying to give me good advice.

It made me wonder if people commonly spend over $20,000–$30,000 a year on rent? And if so, how common is that relative to income and lifestyle choices?

Please look at picrel. Do people spend so much of their paycheque on rent? How does anyone become rich?

Curious what others think about this.

Early replies (first 3)

62372611
>>62372604
>I talked to two people today about rent.
no you didn't

62372621
>$24k per year rent is throwing money down the drain
>$50k per year interest payments for 30 years to "own" the same thing is building wealth

Lol

Lmao

62372624
>>62372604

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62372643
>>62372611
Don’t post in here again. Worthless.
Got nothing to contribute? Don’t post.
>>62372621
The mortgage goes towards ownership. Rent is down the drain.
>>62372624
So you’re unintelligible or cat IQ?

62372649
I didn't read any of this shit

62372651
>>62372649
Yeah, okay, ignore one of the only /biz/ related threads.

62372664
>>62372651
I think I will. This thread is probably gonna suck ass anyway

62372713
>>62372643
>Don’t post in here again.
don't tell me what to do neposchizo

you spam this board with your trash all day.

62372716
>>62372643
Every cent of interest you pay on a mortgage is money down the drain. On a loan for a $1M house you pay another $1M in interest alone.

Rent + investing beats mortgagecucking every time.

62372729
>>62372716
Obviously no one is going to give you a loan for free. Mortgage goes towards ownership. Rent + invest means you own nothing and paying off a rich guy’s mortgage.

62372750
>>62372729
rentlets raging at you OP

62372751
>>62372713
What spam? Read the post. It isn’t spam. It is the most thoughtful well written thread up.
>>62372664
Ngmi. This thread could save your life

62372775
>>62372750
Rentoids seething

62372781
>>62372751
>this thread could save your life
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA
don't make me laugh that's fucking hilarious

62372797
ive never paid more than $1000 a month to live anywhere

item.title: OMGOMGOMGOMG IT HASNT DUMPED WITHIN THE first FULL DAY OF TRADING ITS GOING UP FOREVER AND ALWAYS NO BRAKES ON THIS TRAI… — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
OMGOMGOMGOMG IT HASNT DUMPED WITHIN THE first FULL DAY OF TRADING ITS GOING UP FOREVER AND ALWAYS NO BRAKES ON THIS TRAI…

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-15 23:12 UTC

OP

OMGOMGOMGOMG IT HASNT DUMPED WITHIN THE first FULL DAY OF TRADING ITS GOING UP FOREVER AND ALWAYS NO BRAKES ON THIS TRAIN UP ONLY FOREVER!!! MONEY ISNT REAL. ROCKETS ARE!!!!
>1 TRILLION mARKET CAP
>2 TRILLION MARPET KAP
>U R HER
>3 TRILLIONAIRES
>10 TRILIONIES
>100 TRILLIONZES

Early replies (first 3)

62372684
Strap in, we're going to the moon.

62372691
>>62372673
Can anyone tell me exactly what space x is supposed to be doing?
Mining on the moon?

62372703
>>62372691
I can't believe this needs to be explained but the answer is AI. They said they will build data centers in space and that's all that is needed.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62372710
>>62372691
Mining on mars with robots. No regulatory agencies have the technology to regulate on mars so the sheer number of resources that can be obtained will be substantial. The moon is to be a launchpad.

62372747
>>62372703
>>62372710
>data centers in space
>robot mining on Mars
Amazing, does that happen before or after we get fully autonomous self-driving teslas? Maybe he'll build a hyperloop, but for rockets - that way the minerals are transported faster. I'm so heckin excited guys

62372748
>>62372673
He was right, SpaceX really is driving the goyim insane. They can't handle it and are making threads like this now.

62372768
>>62372691

62372772
>>62372691
lining the pockets of institutional investors

62372779
>>62372747
It looks like famine is next. Anyhoo, the datacenter craze is just a thing to allocate land and utilities to future projects. Most of those won't actually be data centers. A lot of them are going to be neocities. Others to be massive industrial plants. It's just this way they can be allocated funds and space without having to go into details about their data center that isn't a data center project. One of the jokes is that the emissions and usage data used to smear data centers are actually just city data. So it's people arguing against having cities because of the environmental impact.

62372794
>>62372691
SpaceX(and all other X companies which Elon wants to eventually merge together) is trying to become become the irl Wayland Yutani, excluding all the biotech/synth/ayylmao stuff

item.title: (srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb? — 32 replies — 31.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
(srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb?

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-15 22:14 UTC

OP

June 11: chop as market gets ready for pump on spacex trading day

June 12 - June 16: iran FUD is metabolized and market goes up on spacex


June 17 - June 22: FOMC on June 16 EOD causes fud, plus more iran fears

June 22 - Oct 10: NDQ floats upwards 40% while S&P chops and eventually goes up 10%

Oct 10: NDQ reaches a generational top - OpenAI IPOs - Fed announces more hikes due to AI labor inflation - republicans lose congress making trump a lame duck president = no more stimulus grift to pump markets
Oct 10, 2026 - ~Oct 10 2027: as a consequence of the lame duck presidency and the lack of stimulus and the selling of AI venture capital bags onto retail, S&P sells off 40% to round trip all the way back down to 5000

~Oct 10 2027: anthropic ipos

~Oct 10 2027 onwards: pretty much a new 20 year cycle begins and most likely the end of humanity as we birth sentience of machines.

Early replies (first 3)

62349969
you need to factor in the wc which is the biggest npc bread & circus event in modern history

62349972
>>62349960
i just matched up the timelines for 1973 and 2000 btw, that's where I'm getting the play by play for things. I reckon the trading AI's are probably averaging the two together given the macro environment

62349980
>>62349969
ah good point, ya so that might erase the june 17 - june 22 fud period and we go straight into june 22 - oct 10 period

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62350015
>>62349960
Eh. I think they'll just turn shit up to 11 because Bank of America lost.

62352238
biz, so far my prediction is working, stop posting on 1pbtid pepe threads, and let me know if my predictions are shit or not

62353716
snailposting this shit since you retards only want to respond to 1pbtid's

62353764
>>62353716
It'll be a lost decade. It'll take 5-7 years for first new nuclear reactors to come online to unclog the the backlog of ai data centers that will be getting shit canned later this year or next after the crash.

Commodities will be the main play. World needs real resources to build infrastructure and robots. Shit doesn't mine itself out of the ground.

62353866
>>62353764
thanks, yea, seems to be that way. Right around 1973 market peak to 1975ish, gold picked up a 2x bid, so I imagine other commodities would follow suit

It's hard to imagine though, because I look around and I still to this day don't see labor inflation, yet everyone is speaking about how all this build out is happening. If it's happening, why doesn't it show up in labor? That's the first bottleneck that'll show up, no? It showed up in 08, are we saying all the mining and manufacturing just simply doesn't require that many humans? That seems a little bit farfetched to me

62355245
>>62349960
elf

62359675
snailposting this shit since you retards only want to respond to 1pbtid's x2

62359708
Sex with Frieren

62359749
>>62359675
what do you want to hear? the market will go up or go down. your doom pronouncement is stupid since people have been predicting the apocalypse for thousands of years and have always been wrong. also LLMs are gay and AI companies deserve to be buried in piracy and antitrust lawsuits by the next administration. I hope every AI ceo ends up in jail

62359780
>>62359708
Sex with this anon

62359931
>>62359749
fuck yourself, im gonna keep snailposting till you retards give me some concrete nostradamus dates and counter reasoning exactly like you retards do on the 1pbtid pepe threads

62359962
Line will continue up until 7/4/28
7*4 = 28 and they get the satisfaction of destroying a holiday. Will also hand everything to the dems in the next election.

62359991
>>62349960
This is now a Flielen thread

62359998
>>62359991

62361834
snailposting for continued feedback, you're not getting rid of me that easily

62361911
>>62349960
Woah wait a second Frieren, the FOMC is next week? Fucccccccckkkk I need to deleverage.
>FOMC -> NDQ goes up
Walk me through this part if they hike wouldn't that cause a dump or something not more pumping? So should I hold my bullish positions or not? Also I'm pretty sure both Anthropic and OpenAI ipo this year in that order.

62361941
>>62353866
this year I saw a lot of ads for oil related jobs masquerading aa youtube shorts
not sure if that counts

62363423
>>62361911
when trump said he'll be signing the agreement on sunday, the target rates dumped

https://www.cmegroup.com/markets/interest-rates/cme-fedwatch-tool.html

warsh is gonna come out next week and be dovish, but the market will act like its not simply because they're range trading, and they're gonna rebuy their positions.

62364277
motheryuckers, this snail post will exist for years

62364284
>>62349960
>metabolized
Stop reading right there.

62366455
>>62364284

don't worry, illiterate retard opinions aren't needed

62366592
>>62366455
I didn't give an opinion.

62368882
snailposting till nostradamus pomp or domp predictions

62370523
snailposting is just transient guys

62370546
>>62370523
Reddit elf

62370557
>>62370546
tard

62370592
>>62370523

How certain are you about that November election? Rs literally gerrymandered the south and killed D seats + added 12. Trump picks have won every primary solidifying his control. I think it will be a narrow rig in order for the country to not go "troubles" Dem or Rep doesn't fucking matter, probably Rep +1 in the house

62372434
>>62370592
hmm good point, but there's no way they can unfuck the public opinion by november. The pain of inflation will be too fresh, and the memory of Iran causing that pain to boot. All the poor people will be out in force

62372555
>>62359780
Is Frieren a virgin?

Thanks for this thread OP

item.title: So this guy makes the argument that we are a experiencing a fundamental shift in the stock market. — 12 replies — 14.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
So this guy makes the argument that we are a experiencing a fundamental shift in the stock market.

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-15 22:25 UTC

OP

So this guy makes the argument that we are a experiencing a fundamental shift in the stock market.
While previously tech companies used stocks to pay out compensation to their employees and to keep their shareholders happy (through stock buybacks), due to the build out of data centres, they need cash and they are beginning to use stocks to raise capital. SpaceX is merely the first.

This leaves me worried. The bet on AI and data centers seems quiet risky to me, and it looks that the retail investors might be the one left holding the bag, through IPS that are marketed towards retail, and through passive investment in index funds.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wKXgeNwNRJ4

Early replies (first 3)

62372313
Hes wrong, retail won

62372341
>>62372293
That google and apple stacked literal billions in Ireland is true and it's not a secret that they revert that now to invest into AI.
This isn't his take.

What I learned from him is that Musk is confident enough to sell his stocks for millions of retail peeps and cuts into wall street people. Wonder if that will be a theme with Antropic. I don't think it will be that extreme.
The fact that it happened with SpaceX is I think enabled by how Robinhood etc. changed the scene 7 years ago.
Generally, gambling is a huge things now. See Polymarket.

As for AI, I don't think the bubble will pop before the printing starts. I'm looking for the printing, since then cardboard will fly again, even more than now.

62372424
>>62372293
>The bet on AI and data centers seems quiet risky to me
It's less risky when you consider that every data center can replace or add thousands or even millions of office workers, but without any payroll costs. That's why big money is all-in here.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62372432
>>62372424
max IQ to believe this?

62372439
>>62372424
>Replace millions of office workers
>Now mass unemployment
>Nobody has money for anything anymore
>All the businesses can't sell their products anymore

What happens at that point?

62372473
>>62372424
It is not unreasonable to believe that soon enough, local models will be powerful enough to do most of the office work.

62372475
>>62372439
What do you think happens anon? The useless eaters starve while the ultra-rich are coddled by their android caretakers until their eventual demise.

62372476
>>62372341
by printing, are you referring to the printing press? Don't you think the fed will have to raise the interest rate to fight inflation? I am in Europe and that's what's happening here. Dunno how it is in USA

62372491
>>62372476
US can’t raise interest rates without increasing money printing because interest payments on the national debt are now a fifth of government expenditures. They’ll crash interest rates to zero and crater the value of the dollar before they agree to austerity and entitlement reform.

62372513
>>62372491
Currently, the US interest rate is at 3.75%, compared to Europes 2.4%
America shows much more fiscal discipline that Europe.

62372579
>>62372476
>>62372513
I don't have the numbers, but I think the consensus is that the US will eventually print themselves out of their debt.
Raising rates also don't help with debt.
But yes, inflation is the counter-current.

62372597
>>62372293
>use stocks to raise capital
Literally its first use case
/thread

item.title: Law of the jungle — 157 replies — 83.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Law of the jungle

Open thread • Replies: 157 • Updated: 2026-06-15 21:21 UTC

OP

>I am a million billion trillionair-
>BAM BAM BAM
>STAB STAB STAB
W-WHY AM I DYING I AM RICH AND HAVE MONEY ROCKETS CARS AIEEEEEE
P-PLEASE SIRE SAVE M-
>BAM BAM BAM
NOOOO WHY IS MONEY USELESS WHY WHY WHY WAAAAH PLEASE SIR
I WILL GIVE YOU EVERYTHIN-
>STAB HIS KIDS
AAAAAAH MY KIDDIES MY BLOODLINE WHY WHY
PLEASE SIR THE TRILLION MONEY
ACK

Early replies (first 3)

62359354
>>62359336
a mentally buck busted normie is going to say """the law""" grants the rich physical immunity.... and even if it doesn't, they have like, body guards who are omnipotent, omniscient, and omnipresent. you can't do ANYTHING to the rich!!!! it's literally impossible!!!!!

62359360
>>62359336
We live in capitalism and I'm protected by the pigs u need to learn how to move nigga

62359387
>nigger tier envy thread
>/biz/

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62368461
>>62365659

62369006
>>62359336
Ok but why would you do that? Because you are jealous? Or did the rich person rape your mother or something?

62369012
>>62359336
Violence is an incredibly brown phenomen. Just vote socialists into power who will pass tax increases on the rich.

62369030
>>62365772
If we’re being honest here, everyone knows that national socialism type thing is the best system. It doesn’t even need to be tied to just one ethnicity (and nation states actually almost always have different ethnicities living in them, in Germany for example southern germans are mixed with celts and eastern germans with slavs). We could easily have EU level social democracy with tightly regulated, ”only pick the best” kind of immigration.

62369034
>>62369030
*best system for common people i mean. For the elites, USA kind of system with international tax havens is the best.

62369216
>>62359336
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62369231
Let's just recognize this as the pure envy posting that it is. Do nothing drug addicted troon is mad that somebody who is responsible for funding the lives of who knows how many hundreds of thousands of people/families by creating industry and jobs is mad that this person has earned more capital with which they will doubtless continue providing jobs. OP probably personally benefits in ways they aren't even aware of.

62369550
>>62359336
>>62359417
>>62359496
>>62364075
cute

62370324
>>62368461
Reddit is still cringe but unironically got pretty redpilled and revolutionary yeah
They seem more likely do something than 4chan chuds

62370331
>>62359336
cope

62370338
>>62370331
The real cope is asslicking the powerful and kicking down. Wanting to overthrow your slavers is the next step towards liberty at least

62370434
>>62370324
>>62370338
>reddit
>redpilled
>not wanting to destroy literally everything is asslicking
If you are so absolutely hell bent on making this fantasy a reality, why don't you just go start a commune and make it happen? Why does it need to involve murdering people, gaining literally no part of their immense 'stolen' wealth, and giving nothing back to the supposed oppressed workers?
If the utopia can only be built with violence, it will only be upheld with ultraviolence. You won't be the most violent or vicious party around, BTW.

62370741
>>62370434
how do you think our current society is upheld?

62370770
Lmao actual cryptokikes and kikes defending the rich

62370820
>>62361047
>rich
>intelligence
Only room in your bag for one, I'm afraid.

62370830
>>62362126
>let the smart people do the things that make society go
This is what speculators actually believe.

62371364
>>62370741
>upheld
It....isn't?
We had a wildly successful capitalistic system in the 'west' for the last 300 years, and a facsimile of one for the last century (thanks smoke and mirrors fiat), with increasingly onerous compliance demands by the state that start to decouple free markets toward state operated markets (which is far closer to the current paradigm). State regulated markets fucking suck and are a constantly changing landscape of chosen winners and losers based on which group is in power at any one time. Markets are wavering and unstable, currencies are starting to decouple, and global stability is starting to crack under the weight of unnatural (government mandated) migration and capital flows.
Don't ask disingenuous questions, for they open a two way window into your mind (which isn't a compliment)

62371408
>>62359336
thats why we gate keep niggers like that away from us via invitation only or simply not walking near them or anywhere around them your response? ever see batman?

62371411
>>62359336

62371435
>>62365552
I made it. From living in a 1975 Ford maverick to having land and houses and a wife and 3 kids. Go fucking jump, you're a useless eater and only a burden to the rest of us. Fucking pathetic faggot.

62371440
>>62371408
Yeah stay in your house Timmy
Don't let us see you in any cities or public areas

62371441
>>62365566

62371449
>>62371435

62371454

62371459

WONT
DO
SHIT

62371463

62371468

item.title: Did Elon Musk Know the Iran Deal Was Coming? — 10 replies — 29.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Did Elon Musk Know the Iran Deal Was Coming?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-15 22:54 UTC

OP

>The timing is hard to ignore. SpaceX's historic IPO launched trading on June 12th, two days before the Trump administration announced a peace agreement with Iran that sent markets soaring, and that sequence may not be coincidental. The theory goes like this: the Iran war, for all its apparent chaos in the press, was operating on a quiet internal deadline that the administration had set as far back as February, with mid-June as the outer limit for wrapping up the conflict. The public back-and-forth between Iran's foreign minister crying foul over "maximalist demands" and Lindsey Graham expressing concern on the day of the announcement was negotiating theater, not a reflection of what was actually happening behind closed doors. The real agreement was likely reached in early June, with two weeks of runway used for final language, internal political management, and choosing the right public moment. That's also when someone in the administration, in all likelihood, told Musk the plan. SpaceX is not a normal company. It's a $1.75 trillion national security asset, deeply embedded with DoD and US intelligence, and there's a legitimate government interest in its IPO going well. Musk, armed with a specific date, then steered the IPO's still-flexible timeline to land on June 12th: one trading day before the weekend the deal went public, perfectly positioned to catch the rally. None of this is provable. But the alternative asks a lot of coincidence: that the largest IPO in stock market history just happened to land in the two-day window before a major geopolitical resolution.

Early replies (first 3)

62372274
henlo i wrote this think piece
what do u think?

62372277
>>62372263
are we ignoring the 53 times trump has announced a deal before and it didn't happen?

62372281
>>62372263
Pretty sure the IPO was in the investment bank pipeline before the Iran conflict even began.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62372307
>>62372277
Yes

62372321
I think its more likely trump was aware of the largest ipo in history and signed to not fuck it

62372333
>>62372263
I don’t even need to read all of that to know the answer is yes

62372336
>>62372274
This image brings me hope

62372351
>>62372263
There was plenty of signal that it was only a matter of time. For weeks, there were reports of unpaid work and possible water rationing in iran. I get the counterpoint that the regime and their supporters view this as a holy war but the most loyal supporters will eventually capitulate once they start to starve.

62372381
>>62372274
Reads like (and probably is) AI slop

62372743
>>62372274
Very interesting and spot on analysis fren

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 100 replies — 23.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 100 • Updated: 2026-06-15 18:56 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62355133
>>62354803
That’s the part a lot of people overlook. If TBVs actually work as intended, I think people are underestimating how much TVL native BTC use cases could pull in.

62355136
>>62355050
Time will tell whether it was the right call, but at least you acted according to your move.

62355617
>>62355133
It's crazy that DeFi security is still one of the biggest problems in the space.

62355631
>>62354827
Buy avax today and sell @ $100

62355632
>>62339417
Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
And monero is based on bitcoin. The entire crypto market exists because of all the money in bitcoin which is now backed by the US government.

62356269
>>62355617
At this point, thorough audits and multiple layers of review should be the standard, not a nice bonus anon.

62356538
>>62355631
What's your basis?

62357046
>>62345600
Probably the better route. We've seen too many centralized services blow up over the years, so you can't really blame people for preferring fully decentralized setups.

62357057
>>62355632
What about ZEC?

62357077
ICP

62357115
>>62339328
I sold at 116k I dont regret it at all and have about 2 mil waiting for sept-dec buy in.

62357126
>>62339328
I can never regret buying BTC desu, I bought back in around 70K and will DCA more if price goes below 60K. I'm also staking a little BTC on babylon for some extra yield

62357923
>>62355632
>Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
yeah just like the bitcoin developers right? they're the ones who control bitcoin?

it's a good question though, what if they were? miners would have to agree on consensus on which code to accept. if github ever refused to host monero's code then it would just move to .onion or .i2p

62359525
>>62357046
That's how it goes. They've already got GoMining, Ledger, and a16z involved, so let's see who they bring in next.

62359541
>>62357057
ZEC is interesting. If the privacy narrative picks up again, it's usually one of the first names people bring up.

62359557
>>62339328
I literally don't care about it. Mogged by virtually everything else over the last couple of years.

Also why is faggot shit like dogecoin still worth billions of dollars.

>in b4 bottom signal
Probably.

62359588
>>62357126
Sounds like you've got a plan and you're sticking to it. Hard to ask for much more than that. I wouldn't be surprised if more people follow suit over time. The $4B+ TVL shows there's already plenty of interest.

62359608
>>62359557
The market has a weird habit of rewarding the things nobody can rationally explain.

62359673
>>62357077
People still remember the launch, but the tech discussion around ICP is often more interesting than the price chart.

62360479
>>62359557
When bottom

62361782
>>62360479
Nobody knows

62362657
>>62359557
How do you mean?

62362661
>>62339328
While BTC is in deep bear stage, it's time to get in. I need to be greedy when others are fear.

62363445
>>62362657
It's his opinion

62364599
>>62339349
>Time to start acting like an african american.
finally someone said it

62365449
>>62362661
You're just doing the right thing..

62365460
any time you can cash out, turn a profit and not get anally blasted by exchanges is a good time to cash out.

62368338
>>62364599
Damn

62369180
Obviously my >$100k sells were the "right move". Cycle theory helped a lot with that one. If I had the clarity of hindsight, I would have sold more. Bitcoin is amazing tech but its potential is as-yet unrealized. Holding it doesn't do much for anyone by itself. Sure, people say you can borrow against it or maybe get yield somewhere - fuck that shit. I need to see a thriving system of exchange based on Bitcoin replacing the current economy before I keep it around for practical reasons.
Until that point, I trade dollars for Bitcoin, Bitcoin for more dollars, and dollars for more Bitcoin. How I trade it is based on the likelihood of reaching that final evolutionary stage, and right now it's looking pretty dire. What the establishment wants is to turn Bitcoin into a exotic monetary instrument held for bragging points and dick measurement reasons, and that's what people like Saylor and ETFs are doing, and why OGs are dumping on their faces. What I want to see more of is trustless stateless peer-to-peer transactions involving real shit like commodities, cars, houses etc. which seems to be happening through Monero.

Anyways I'm hoping to buy back lower around August-September. Maybe $35-40k. Thanks for reading my diary.

62371627
>>62365460
time will tell.

62371786
>>62371768

62371874
>>62371786

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62371936
>>62371874
respect

62372337
>>62371746
Do tell the signal.

62372348
>>62372337
Rates up = everything else down. Simple.

62372362
>>62372348
Shorting the bottom. Biz never fails.

62372383
>>62372362
nah too much hype in everything.
wait for the next clown-TACO and we see everything south super quick!

>dont believe in the art-of-the-deal(c)(R)

62372388
>>62372383
If there's too much hype in everything then there will be a flight to safety

62372398
>>62372383
Unless are we saying the same thing? Sell stocks buy bonds?

62372653
>>62372398
If rates are going up that means bonds are getting dumped as the buyers would rather buy bonds with a higher rate of return to them.

62372671
>>62372653
I don't think rates will rise

62366599

62366630
>>62365540
the only girls that care about careers in 2026 are egg freezing hags. you need just enough money to get to where women are (like the bar or anywhere else) and live somewhere. anything past that is diminishing returns.

62366635
anon don't listen to desperate insecure virgins for dating advice
the world is full of poor women who will date you if you have a good personality
when my wife and I started dating, I was literally unemployed and living with my parents
Now I make more than her but it took me 5 years to get there.

62366639
>>62365555
>I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection

Dad?

62366677
>>62365608
all of these women made a different man spend thousands and wait months before fucking really makes you ponder

62366728
>>62366599
well eventually these kinds of women also tend to be single mothers

62367007
>>62365897
Yes paradoxically the girls that are most wife material aren't the ones going around looking to date. I think if you are out of school you're out of luck
>Girlfriend through connections
I know this sounds stupid, but I'd feel like a cuck if I met my girlfriend through a mutual.
Think about it, MY girlfriend would have been interested in talking to someone else FIRST. I wouldn't want to date a girl with guy friends anyways

Impossible propositions are abound

62367017
>>62366635
Do the math.

62367050
>>62367007
Yeah, that’s pretty retarded.
There are plenty of non-sexual reasons why a guy might know some other girl, like a family member, girlfriend/wife’s friend, former co-worker, former school friend.
You could make friends with girls to try to date one of their friends too.
The crux is on your ability to be a normal person to any normal person regardless of gender or age.
That’s how you find girls who aren’t sluts and divorce rapists. Not how you sleep around though.
So you have to figure out if what you really want is someone to have sex with regularly, or someone to start a family with. Because it’s a lot of social effort.

62367055
>>62367007
I just realized, you may have severely low self esteem if you can’t handle the thought of a gf talking to someone else before you.
Low self esteem is girl repellant
Fix that first

62367080
>>62365555
Checked and damn feels bad, I hope you make it.

>>62365540
Former sex haver here, if she loves you not much. Unironically you could go have a picnic with some bullshit like pizza, or go on a road trip. Strategic use of money is ideal. Don't just buy flowers, get them delivered to her work so all her coworkers get pissed and jealous. You want enough money to lock down a nice wedding and kids but thats really it, this was my fatal mistake. I have enough for three weddings now but opportunities are gone for me.

62367870

If not a demo thread, you're overthinking it retard

Also if you're so insecure talk to a therapist/take shrooms and start working out. Its all ego that you have to let go in the end.

Good luck fren!

62371972

>Also girls can have guy friends that they don't wanna fuck.
even if this is the case the guy most likely wants to fuck them you retard.

part of being in a relationship is having a basic level of respect for your partner. that means dropping all single behaviour, so no hanging out 1 on 1 with the opposite sex, no texting other guys, and no getting drunk without you in bars/clubs. its not insecurity to expect a the most basic of relationship boundaries.

>just take drugs bro!
>go pay money to a millenial woman
